t_en: Let's look for the next one!

t_ch: 家人，你终于来了！要是缺了你，咱们这支「捉鬼小队」可就缺了一个强大战力！
t_en: Yo, what's up! You're finally here! Without you, our ghost-hunting squad would be nothing!

t_ch: 论战斗，我也是很靠谱的啊！
t_en: Hey, I'm a reliable squad member too!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，是啊，裳裳你醒着的时候最靠谱了！
t_en: Sure, when you're not passed out in fear! Hehe...

t_ch: 小桂子，你要是再讽刺我…我、我就再也不配合你表演胸口碎大石了！
t_en: Cut it out — if you keep teasing me, I—I... I'm not gonna perform my boulder-smashing trick for you anymore!

t_ch: 这捉鬼小队是怎么回事？
t_en: What's this ghost-hunting squad all about?

t_ch: 记录在案正确的名称应当是「十王司下辖岁阳逃逸事件特别处理行动小组」。
t_en: The official name is the "Ten-Lords Commission Special Operations Team for the Heliobi Containment Breach Incident."

t_ch: 这是由十王颁旨所成立的队伍名目。想邀请前不久绥园灾异事件后安然无恙的几位，以各位最擅长的方式寻找并镇伏岁阳。
t_en: That was the name given to us by decree of the Ten Lords. Since we managed to survive the Fyxestroll Garden incident, the Ten Lords decided to entrust us with hunting down and subduing the heliobi using our special skills.

t_ch: 十王颁旨？
t_en: By decree of the Ten Lords?

t_ch: 嗯，此事是由十王亲自下诏促成。虽然我并没有得到更多理由，但多半也能猜到其中的原因。
t_en: That's right — this expedition was commissioned by the Ten Lords themselves. I am unsure as to why they attached so much importance to this endeavor, but I have a theory.

t_ch: 以我们最擅长的方式？
t_en: Using our special skills?

t_ch: 藿藿是判官，我是云骑，十王司有调遣责无旁贷。{NICKNAME}嘛，也是为罗浮出过力流过汗的天外友人……
t_en: Huohuo is a judge of the Ten-Lords Commission and I'm a Cloud Knight — we must answer the call to the best of our abilities. And {NICKNAME} is an outworlder who's done great things for the sake of the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 但怎么把小桂子这样的群众也给拉下水了？这多危险！
t_en: But why did Little Gui have to get dragged into this? This mission is too dangerous for a civilian!

t_ch: …我明白了，你是嫌我帮不上忙，只会给你们添乱是吧？
t_en: ...I get it. You don't think I have any special skills, so I'll just get in the way?

t_ch: 仙舟上一花一草皆有意义，桂小姐自然有她的过人之处：身为抛头露面的街头艺人，网络上的主播，她非常清楚如何扩展咱们需要的消息来源。
t_en: Every flower and tree on the Xianzhou is significant in its own way — Miss Gui has her own strengths too. As a famous street performer and livestreamer, she has precisely the skills we need to acquire intel for our mission.

t_ch: 我没问题了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 我已劳烦桂小姐在某个网站上设立了账号，以解决诡事奇闻为由头，为各位行动提供信源。
t_en: Miss Gui has set up a social media account under the guise of investigating paranormal phenomena and unexplained mysteries... we can use it to gather intel for our mission.

t_ch: 裳裳，听见了吗！这回的我岂止是有用，简直是有用。能有机会作为十王司的鹰犬帮上大家的忙，真是太好了！
t_en: You hear that, Sushang! I'm a key member of the squad! They said I could be a Ten-Lords Commission intelligence hawk!

t_ch: 呃，虽然我没什么文化，但「鹰犬」不是什么好词儿吧？
t_en: Uh... I might not be a scholar, but I don't think calling someone a "hawk" is a compliment.

t_ch: 判官藿藿受命调遣各位，在岁阳相关的专业问题上，请你们务必配合她的指挥。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has specially entrusted Judge Huohuo with commanding the ghost-hunting squad, so please make sure to follow her lead in all matters related to the heliobi.

t_ch: 我…我明白了，藿藿一定会不辱使命的。
t_en: ...Th—That's right. I won't let us down.

t_ch: 寒鸦交待完捉鬼小队的事，便离开继续处理绥园事务了。
t_en: After Hanya delivered the task to the ghost-hunting squad, she left to continue resolving matters at Fyxestroll Garden.

t_ch: 藿藿大人，咱们开始行动吧？
t_en: Captain Huohuo, should we start the ghost hunt now?

t_ch: 大、大人？你还是叫我藿藿吧。
t_en: C—Captain? You can just call me Huohuo.

t_ch: 好的藿藿大人。
t_en: Understood, Captain Huohuo.

t_ch: 寒鸦小姐让我来带领大家，我也很意外。
t_en: I'm really surprised that Madam Hanya asked me to be the leader of the ghost-hunting squad.

t_ch: 尾巴消失以后，我本以为自己会被辞退的。但不知为何……总之，我一定尽力而为，不给大家拖后腿！
t_en: After Tail disappeared, I thought for sure the Commission would ask me to resign, but for some reason... A—Anyway, I'll do my best to carry out my duties!

t_ch: 全看你的啦，判官。
t_en: We're all counting on you, Judge Huohuo.

t_ch: 其实，我也不知道现在的我能不能帮上各位的忙……
t_en: Actually, I'm not sure if I can be helpful anymore...

t_ch: 尾巴消失以后，我本以为自己会被辞退的。但不知为何…寒鸦小姐让我来带领大家，我也很意外。
t_en: After Tail disappeared, I thought for sure the Commission would ask me to resign... but for some reason, Madam Hanya asked me to be the leader of the ghost-hunting squad.

t_ch: 总之，我一定尽力而为，不给大家拖后腿！
t_en: A—Anyway, I'll do my best to carry out my duties!

t_ch: 言归正传…除了被镇伏在绥园里的浮烟，洪炉破碎后，还有不少岁阳逃逸在外。
t_en: As for our mission... Cirrus was locked up in Fyxestroll Garden, but there were a lot of other heliobi that escaped after the Creation Furnace was broken.

t_ch: 按照十王司对岁阳习性的理解，它们多半会往人居稠密的地带寻找宿主。所到之处一定会有许多不能解释的「灵异事件」。
t_en: According to the Ten-Lords Commission's understanding of heliobi behavior, they tend to roam densely populated areas in search of hosts... and wherever they go, there are bound to be reports of "supernatural phenomena."

t_ch: 桂小姐，能否展示一下那个网站？
t_en: Little Gui, can you show us the website we'll be using for intel?

t_ch: 「罗浮杂…」小桂子，这「罗浮杂蛆」是个什么网站啊？怎么叫这么个名字？
t_en: Let's see... Ghast—ly Grayv... ee? Ghastly Gravy!? What kind of website is that?

t_ch: …那叫「罗浮杂俎」！行不行啊你。
t_en: Ugh, it's Ghostly Grove, not Ghastly Gravy!

t_ch: 「罗浮杂俎」是罗浮仙舟上规模最大的灵异爱好者网站，所有喜欢怪力乱神的罗浮人都知道它。
t_en: Ghostly Grove is the most renowned website for paranormal enthusiasts on the Luofu. Everyone who loves the mysterious and the unexplained knows about it.

t_ch: 网站上一直有人在分享自己的灵异经历，我之前也常去那上面看「见鬼实录」来锻炼胆量……
t_en: I used to go there to read first-hand accounts of ghost sightings whenever I needed to build up my courage.

t_ch: 我们想借这个网站获取信息，再打着解决灵异事件的幌子暗中调查。
t_en: We can get our leads from this website and then investigate them posing as paranormal investigators.

t_ch: 毕竟冥差大张旗鼓挨家挨户的搜索，不但效率低下，还会引起人们惊慌，激化岁阳对宿主的控制。
t_en: That means we can operate more efficiently, and with a low profile... If people started to see a spiritfarer making door-to-door visits, they might begin to panic... and that would give the heliobi even more power over their hosts.

t_ch: 因为我刚注册，粉丝还很少。根据我在其他平台的经验，等到粉丝多起来，就会有很多人来贡献各种灵异事件的消息。
t_en: Look, I'm a new user, so I only have a few followers on this site... but based on past experience, once you get a following, people start flooding you with stories and news about paranormal activity.

t_ch: 万一只是谣言怎么办？
t_en: What if all the leads we get turn out to be fake?

t_ch: 装神弄鬼多半都是人为的。
t_en: A lot of paranormal activity is just people in costumes.

t_ch: 其实还挺难的。有的信息一眼假，但有的信息嘛…仅凭网上的只言片语恐怕很难分辨。
t_en: We have our work cut out for us, that's for sure. Sometimes a lead is obviously fake, but other times... it's hard to tell from just a few words on the Internet.

t_ch: 毕竟有些人为了吸引流量，真是什么瞎话都能编得出来。
t_en: After all, some people will make up all kinds of nonsense to attract attention.

t_ch: 所以还是得亲自上前线确认咯？
t_en: So we still have to go out and fact-check the leads?

t_ch: 嗯，不止咱们几个，十王司的冥差们也会暗行调查。
t_en: Mm. Plus, the spiritfarers from the Ten-Lords Commission will be carrying out their own investigation in secret as well.

t_ch: 吸引关注…小桂子倒是很擅长，她平日里经常用我来表演杂技什么的…但在这灵异爱好者网站上，该怎么涨粉呢？
t_en: Speaking of attracting attention... how do we get a following on a paranormal activity website? Sounds like Little Gui's department — she used me in some of her performances just to draw a bigger crowd.

t_ch: 简单，咱们把捉鬼……呃我是说「镇伏岁阳」的过程实录发出去就好了！我们现在可是给十王司当差，谁手上还能有比我们更劲爆的料啊？
t_en: That's the easy part — we just post some videos of us ghost hunting... Uh, I mean, subduing heliobi! We're working for the Ten-Lord's Commission after all — we're gonna have the best content out there!

t_ch: 这十王司能答应吗？
t_en: Will the Ten-Lords agree to this?

t_ch: 那位寒鸦小姐同意了呀。我和她说，我打算利用捉岁阳涨粉，再利用粉丝捉岁阳，形成一个正向生态循环…她说没问题。
t_en: Madam Hanya has already given us the green light. We'll record our heliobi-hunting expeditions to get more followers, and then use the intel from our followers to find more heliobi. It's like a vicious cycle... W—Wait — a virtuous cycle!

t_ch: 这么做确实高效。
t_en: Does sound kinda effective...

t_ch: 等等，这样一来岂不是违背了隐秘行事的原则？
t_en: Wait, aren't we supposed to be operating in secret?

t_ch: 而且岁阳以情绪为食，往往会操纵受害者暴露内心隐藏的一面…
t_en: Another thing we have to think about is that heliobi feed on the emotions of their hosts. They can even manipulate a host into revealing their deepest secrets...

t_ch: 把受害者的信息发到网上，会不会…造成一些二次伤害？我我我绝对没有批评你的意思…只是提出一些担忧。
t_en: Wouldn't posting a victim's personal information online... cause even more harm to the victim? I—I'm not trying to pick holes... just raising some concerns.

t_ch: 我的看法是，「隐秘行事」与「曝光灵异」可不冲突，倒不如说，这是同一件事。
t_en: The way I see it, "operating in secret" and "exposing the paranormal" go hand-in-hand.

t_ch: 隐去受害人姓名，只留下咱们几个镇伏鬼怪的英姿，拍些激烈战斗的留影，再配个带点刺激性的标题……
t_en: We won't reveal the names of any victims. We'll just post some pics and videos of the squad engaging in fierce battles with the supernatural! And then come up with some dramatic titles...

t_ch: 比如什么《阿芬探灵：云骑美少女夜路遇鬼，只用三秒钟她让妖魔后悔》。
t_en: Like: "Little Gui Investigates: Ghost Tries to Scare Young Cloud Knight Girl on Creepy Road — Gets Owned..." Y'know?

t_ch: 抓捕岁阳的行动被咱们渲染成一个个抓人眼球的娱乐视频。人们也会对类似的事件半信半疑，恐慌情绪一下子就消失了！
t_en: We'll turn the entire heliobi-hunting process into regular uploads. Viewers will see that there's nothing that scary about it, and there won't be any public panic!

t_ch: 没完了是吧，怎么又拐到我这来了！不过，我承认你说的很有道理。
t_en: Why are you looking at me? Want a medal or something...? It does kinda sound like a good idea...

t_ch: 多谢损友支持哈！
t_en: Hee-hee, thanks bestie.

t_ch: 当然啦，我知道大家对这个新型捉鬼方式不是很信任…那你们稍等一下啊，我来翻翻情报，咱们几人挑一个试试。
t_en: I feel like some of you aren't convinced by the whole online ghost-hunting thing... How about this — we take a look at the leads we have so far, and pick one to try out as a test run.

t_ch: 《偶然拍到了灵异照片》，这背景也太假了…《一直考不过星槎驾照是不是被诅咒了？》，别给自己找借口了……
t_en: "When You See It You'll Scream!" That's so cliche, with the arrow and everything... "Failed Your Starskiff Pilot Test? You're Cursed!" Tsk, people are always looking for excuses...

t_ch: 哎，{NICKNAME}，我正在筛选可疑的事件呢，你打算现在就开始咱们捉鬼小队的行动，还是大家伙聊聊天，增进增进了解？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm trying to find some interesting leads. You ready to start ghost hunting, or do you wanna chat with the squad first?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's hunt some ghosts.

t_ch: 说到灵异事件，还真让我找到了一条可疑消息。
t_en: I found a suspicious report...

t_ch: 你们直接过来看吧，这帖子已经有不少回复了。
t_en: Take a look — this post is getting tons of comments.

t_ch: 「白露大人出逃」？这有什么好奇怪的，龙女大人不是三天两头出逃吗？
t_en: Lady Bailu disappeared? That's nothing new — she sneaks off all the time.

t_ch: 所以关键问题不是「出逃」啊，你看下文——
t_en: That's not the point — here, read this part...

t_ch: 「那位丹鼎司医士正要过去抓住白露大人，白露大人竟然凭空消失，让她扑了个空……」
t_en: "One of the healers from the Alchemy Commission had Lady Bailu cornered, but then she disappeared into thin air..."

t_ch: 会不会是看错了？
t_en: Maybe they were seeing things.

t_ch: 会不会是造谣？
t_en: Maybe they made it up.

t_ch: 从跟帖上来看，目击者不在少数。也没人听说过龙女大人会什么逃脱魔术吧？
t_en: Judging from the comments, there were several witnesses. Sure, Lady Bailu runs away all the time, but disappearing into thin air?

t_ch: 确实像是岁阳所为…岁阳本就擅长操弄感官，文献中也常有它们幻化身影，装神弄鬼的记载。
t_en: It looks like the work of a heliobus... The heliobi are masters of manipulating the senses. There are stories of heliobi changing their shape and appearance to disguise themselves as someone else.

t_ch: 那还等什么呢，咱们赶紧行动起来，找到目击者打听打听？
t_en: Okay, so what are we waiting for? Let's get moving and see if we can find some witnesses.

t_ch: 在出发之前，确实应该先做好情报上的准备。
t_en: Before we head out, let's make sure we have our intel in order.

t_ch: 如果能知晓岁阳想做什么，能做什么，对解救被附身的宿主应该会有很大的帮助。
t_en: If we can better understand what a heliobus wants to do — and what it's capable of doing — it'll be easier for us to help the victim.

t_ch: 向当事的目击者打听是个办法，但那些人对岁阳本身一无所知。我、我有个不成熟的想法…咱们手上还扣着另一个情报来源。
t_en: Interviewing witnesses might give us some clues, but they won't know anything about heliobi. I... I think I have an idea... We have another source of intel we could use...

t_ch: 你是说…浮烟？
t_en: You mean... Cirrus?

t_ch: 对喔！那些岁阳在同一个单间里被关了那么久，肯定很了解这些狱友的脾气。
t_en: That's right! Those heliobi were locked up in the same space for ages! They probably got to know their fellow prisoners like the back of their... Huh, I guess they don't really have hands...

t_ch: 浮烟怎么会听我们的？
t_en: Why would Cirrus help us?

t_ch: 我听寒鸦小姐说，这坏东西被阵法困在这儿，正和判官僵着呢。它会配合咱们吗？
t_en: I heard from Miss Hanya that Cirrus is caught in a stalemate — the Evil Binding Matrix has trapped them. Will they really cooperate with us?

t_ch: 虽然一想到要去见浮烟…我、我就怕得要死…不过，我猜它一定很想见到我，吃了我。
t_en: Th—The thought of seeing Cirrus sends shivers down my spine... but I bet Cirrus would be eager to see me again... they'd devour me whole if they could.

t_ch: 它被尾巴摆了一道，心里一直耿耿于怀，只要咱们能激起它的好胜心，就能让它按我们的想法来行事。
t_en: I think Tail made a fool of Cirrus, and Cirrus has a held a grudge ever since. If we can get them worked up over it, we might be able to use them to our advantage.

t_ch: 我打算再和她们聊聊。
t_en: I wanna chat for a bit first.

t_ch: 啊，无聊到令人心碎，连偶尔有飞虫掠过都成了乐趣。但这一次，有几只小飞虫一块找上门来了……
t_en: Ugh, this tedium... even the insects can't hold my attention any longer. I wonder if these ones will prove to be more entertaining?

t_ch: 浮烟，想找你了解点情况，请你配合一下我们的工作。
t_en: Cirrus, we've come to ask you a few questions. I hope you'll be cooperative.

t_ch: 哟，这不是一进绥园就昏了过去的小妹妹吗？几天不见，说话口气就硬成这样了？
t_en: Oh, if it isn't the little girl who fainted in Fyxestroll Garden the moment she encountered the heliobi? Already talking to me like a detective, are you?

t_ch: 嘿！我的剑呢？你你你给我等着……
t_en: Hey! Where's my sword? Y—You just wait...

t_ch: 真的好想揍它…
t_en: Can we destroy Cirrus already?

t_ch: 嗯哼，这园子里想拿我开刀的人多了去了，要不你们商量商量排个先后？
t_en: Hmph, there are plenty of beings in this garden that would like to kill me... Why don't you line up one-by-one and take turns trying?

t_ch: 不、不可以…宿主是无辜的。
t_en: D—Don't be ridiculous... We can't allow the host to be harmed.

t_ch: 别被它挑唆了。
t_en: Don't let them get to you.

t_ch: 你说得对。忍一时风平浪静…退一步越想越气…
t_en: Okay, okay... I just wish I could teach them a lesson!

t_ch: 浮烟…因为一个小小的疏忽落败了，你…很不甘心吧？
t_en: Cirrus... you must be unhappy with how things turned out, right?

t_ch: 小狐狸，你想从我这儿得到什么？想知道那条看门狗的下落？抱歉，它早就灰•飞•烟•灭啦！
t_en: What do you want from me, little fox girl? You want to know what happened to the guard dog? He turned to ashes a long time ago!

t_ch: 我…不关心这些。倒是你…好不容易从洪炉里逃了出来。眼下有的岁阳在罗浮各处逍遥快活。你却要被困在这儿寸步难行…你甘心吗？
t_en: I... I don't care about that at all... but I can't help but notice — you managed to escape the furnace, and now you're stuck here, unable to move an inch, while the other heliobi are roaming around the Luofu enjoying themselves... Must be quite frustrating.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…你想要我帮助你们，出卖同类？
t_en: Hahaha... You want me to assist you and betray my own kind?

t_ch: 浮烟陷入了漫长的沉默，躲在冥差的双眼后，它反复打量着在场的每一个人，最后终于开口——
t_en: Cirrus stays silent, sizing everyone up through the eyes of the spiritfarer they possess. Finally, they decide to talk.

t_ch: 我有一个条件。我想重续当年大岁阳「燎原」与将军的胜负。只要你能把如今罗浮的将军带到我面前…一切都有可谈的余地。
t_en: ...I can do that — under one condition... I wish to arrange a challenge — one akin to the struggle between the great heliobus Ignamar and the general of the Luofu. As long as you can bring me the current general... anything can be negotiated.

t_ch: 将军……？
t_en: The general...?

t_ch: 怎么，这就难倒你了？看来判官大人想和岁阳做交易的决心，还•不•够•啊！
t_en: What, is that too much to ask? It seems the judge here doesn't have what it takes to negotiate with a heliobus!

t_ch: 这家伙张口说的每个字都是在变着法拒绝你……
t_en: That's just another way of saying they don't want to cooperate...

t_ch: 哎？藿藿，你不会真的打算……
t_en: Huh? Wait, Huohuo, you can't seriously be thinking about—

t_ch: 成交，我会去神策府请将军来。
t_en: Deal! I'll submit a request to the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: 嗯？无名客、云骑、十王司判官还有街头艺人…这个组合倒是新奇。
t_en: Oh? What do we have here? A Nameless, a Cloud Knight, a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission, and a street performer? Quite the group.

t_ch: 呀，将军大人居然认得我吗？没想到我竟然这么有名气了！
t_en: Wow, the general recognizes me? I didn't realize I was so famous!

t_ch: 哈哈，听我的侍卫彦卿提过。你可是罗浮上少有能令他失去佩剑，空手而返的人。
t_en: Haha, my retainer, Yanqing, has mentioned you. You are one of the only people on the Luofu that was able to deprive him of his sword.

t_ch: 我…我想起来了！真是对不住对不住。一时兴起表演了吞剑，结果散场时竟然忘了把那柄剑还给小哥。
t_en: Huh? Oh! I—I remember... I should apologize — I did a sword-swallowing performance on the spur of the moment, but forgot to return the sword to him when I left the stage...

t_ch: 所以，今天吹的是什么风，让几位聚到神策府来找我？
t_en: So, what wind has brought you to the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 藿藿讲起了绥园中发生的岁阳灾异，以及浮烟的要求。
t_en: Huohuo tells Jing Yuan about the heliobi crisis and Cirrus' demand.

t_ch: 将军日理万机，十王司本不该拿岁阳的事情来打扰您。但任由那些妖物到处徘徊，恐怕……
t_en: General, we know you have important matters to attend to, and the Ten-Lords Commission shouldn't be bothering you with something as trivial as the heliobi... but if we allow those creatures to roam freely, I'm afraid...

t_ch: 嗯，我曾听闻过前代将军与精怪交战的逸话，还以为是随口编成的故事。没想到正主寻仇上门来了，只是找到我头上却有些冤枉了。
t_en: I heard stories of a skirmish between a previous general and a monster of sorts — I'd always assumed they were an old wives' tale. I never imagined the villain of such tales would come to my doorstep seeking revenge. I must say, it seems a bit unfair that I should assume the debt for a conflict I had no involvement in.

t_ch: 冤有头，债有主，
t_en: It wasn't your fight to begin with.

t_ch: 话说的不错！本着「谁污染，谁治理」的原则确该让当事人出马。但是……
t_en: Precisely! In principle, the instigator should bear the burden. Still...

t_ch: 该出您这个奇兵了！
t_en: Masterstroke them! Please?

t_ch: 看来{NICKNAME}是铁了心要我奉还人情了。
t_en: It would appear you are eager to see me assume the debt.

t_ch: 腾骁将军是我敬重的前辈，我克绍箕裘，自然不能旁观坐视。人家指明了要见罗浮的将军，那我就会会它。
t_en: General Teng Xiao is a venerated predecessor. If I am to honor his legacy, then sitting idly by is not an option. If an audience with the general of the Luofu is requested, then I will honor such a request.

t_ch: 将军，岁阳这邪物极其擅长占夺凡人的肉体，如果您有顾虑……
t_en: General, heliobi are vile creatures capable of taking over the bodies of mortals... If you're unsure...

t_ch: 多谢十王司为我考虑。我虽不擅长对付妖魔鬼怪，但岁阳逃逸，祸及仙舟黎庶，身为将军的我责无旁贷。
t_en: I appreciate the Ten-Lords Commission having such high regard for my welfare. It is true I am rather inexperienced when it comes to heliobi, but as general of the Luofu, I cannot look on while these fugitive creatures wreak havoc on the populace.

t_ch: 几位不妨先行一步，待批了手头的公文，我会立刻前往绥园。
t_en: I still have some paperwork that needs attending to — once I am finished, I will make my way to Fyxestroll Garden without delay.

t_ch: 我没在园子里见过你…人类，你是谁？
t_en: I have never seen this one before... Human, who are you?

t_ch: 岁阳，你不是心心念念想要重续与将军的战斗么？我应邀而来。
t_en: Heliobus, is it not your deepest wish to hold a rematch with the general of the Luofu? I have come to grant you an audience.

t_ch: 这位便是当代将军！
t_en: He's the present-day general!

t_ch: 这位是腾骁将军…的继任者！
t_en: This is General Teng Xiao's successor!

t_ch: 浮烟，你想见的人，我们已为你请来了。如今坐镇神策府的，便是这位景元将军。
t_en: Cirrus, we have brought you the person you wish to see. This is General Jing Yuan, the one who now sits at the Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: …这就是当代的将军？
t_en: ...This is the current general of the Luofu?

t_ch: 一副刚刚睡醒的样子，看起来弱爆了好嘛。腾骁呢？他怎么选了这么个家伙来接任？不过，让仙舟人比武招选，重新决出个将军来也不现实。
t_en: Look at that expression — he's barely half-awake! Where is General Teng Xiao? Why did he choose such a lightweight to take over for him? I suppose it's already too late for the Xianzhou to rid themselves of this clown...

t_ch: 那么，景元将军，咱们就在这绥园里一决胜负如何？！若我获胜，就任我自由来去——
t_en: Very well then, General Jing Yuan — how about we settle our score right here in Fyxestroll Garden? If I win, I must be free to come and go as I please...

t_ch: 我只说「应邀而来」，却从没答应要和你一战。
t_en: I agreed to grant you an audience, not meet you in combat.

t_ch: …什么，你不应战？你不应战？！
t_en: ...What? You refuse? You won't fight me!?

t_ch: 他说为了重续当年的战斗而来到此处，却不应战，难道是变相承认我不战而胜？不对，在人类的逻辑里，不战和胜利不能划上等号……
t_en: He comes here after I demand a rematch, and won't even fight... Is he conceding that I already won? No, there can be no victory in eschewing combat — human logic negates it...

t_ch: 你误会了，我确实是为当年之战而来，只是…你还不配。
t_en: You misunderstand — I came for a rematch. It's just that... you are not a worthy opponent.
WHAT!? UNWORTHY!?

t_ch: 腾骁身为罗浮的将军，节制云骑；而燎原则是岁阳之首，号令群魔；二者如天覆地载，阴阳相薄，双方龙战实在是合情合理，合乎规矩。
t_en: Teng Xiao, as general of the Luofu, was commander-in-chief of the Cloud Knights. Ignamar commanded the heliobi. The two were as great as the sky and the earth — equally matched like yin and yang. It is only natural that they were worthy opponents.

t_ch: 而眼下，我乃是仙舟的将军，你却已不再是燎原，你不过是一介系狱囚犯。要想与我交战，须得按我的规矩，击败我的军队，杀到中军帐前，我才给你这个机会。
t_en: As it stands, I am the general of the Luofu... but you are no Ignamar. Merely a prisoner. If you wish to engage me in battle, you must follow my rules. Defeat my army — only then will I grant you the fight you seek.

t_ch: …你的军队？
t_en: ...Your army?

t_ch: 没错，以此高台为限，你要击败我身后这四位朋友。
t_en: That's right. You must defeat my four companions gathered here — then you will have proved yourself a worthy opponent.

t_ch: 哎？我们？没没没没问题…吗？
t_en: Wait... he means us!? W—W—W—We'll be okay, right?

t_ch: 我们能有什么问题。
t_en: We got this.

t_ch: 是啊，能靠武力解决的问题都不是问题。
t_en: That's right! For us, any problem that can be solved by force isn't a problem at all.

t_ch: 怎么突然让我们打架…
t_en: That escalated quickly...

t_ch: 打就打呗，能靠武力解决的问题都不是问题。
t_en: We'll be fine! For us, any problem that can be solved by force isn't a problem at all.

t_ch: 到最后，奇兵还是我啊…
t_en: Can we practice our masterstrokes first?

t_ch: 我年纪轻轻，将军怎么对我这么有信心啊？
t_en: Why is the general putting his trust in us? I'm too young to die!

t_ch: …只要打败这区区四个卒子？
t_en: ...So I only need to beat your pawns here?

t_ch: 你可不要小看他们四人，这四位各个身怀绝技，斗志与耐性也是技惊四座。我只怕你拾掇不下。
t_en: It would serve you well not to underestimate them. They are as skilled in combat as they are tenacious in spirit. I worry they might pose a problem for you.

t_ch: 若你赢了，我便重续当年的对决。若是输了，你要回答他们一个问题——不可说谎，不可狡辩。成交？
t_en: If you win, you can have your rematch. If you lose, you will answer one question of their choice — no lies, no tricks. Can you accept these terms?

t_ch: 成交！就让我们以此高台为枰，以人为子，来一番较量！
t_en: It's a deal! The game is set — this platform will be our chessboard, and the fighters will be the pieces!

t_ch: 你输了，浮烟。
t_en: You've lost, Cirrus.

t_ch: 别得意，如果我拥有燎原的所有力量…怎么会败给你们！
t_en: Hmph, don't get complacent — if I had all of Ignamar's power, I'd never have lost to the likes of you!

t_ch: 胜败兵家事未期。若你想再来过，我可以随时奉陪，等候你不断挑战。当然，得赢过这四位才行。
t_en: Should you decide to try again, my offer still stands... but you must defeat them.

t_ch: 那就再来！
t_en: Then what are we waiting for!

t_ch: 且慢！在开始下一场战斗前，你要按约回答十王司一个问题。
t_en: Not so fast! Before we fight again, you need to answer one question, like you promised.

t_ch: 讨厌的小鬼…你问吧！
t_en: Pesky devil of a girl... Alright, what's your question?

t_ch: 你瞧瞧，这件事有没有可能是岁阳所为——
t_en: Look... is this something a heliobus could have done?

t_ch: 你们向浮烟展示了白露在金人巷突然失踪的传闻。
t_en: You show Cirrus the post about Bailu's sudden disappearance.

t_ch: 被封禁在洪炉里的岁阳多得很，每个都有可能。出于报复，出于好奇，出于饥饿…我怎么知道会是哪个拐走了你们的龙女？
t_en: There are countless heliobi that escaped from the Creation Furnace, and every one of them could have done such a thing. It could have been vengeance, curiosity, or simply hunger... So how would I know which one abducted your Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 不过，怀疑的对象，倒也不是没有。比如阿灼……
t_en: Of course, I do have my suspicions. For example, Luxie...

t_ch: 阿灼是个什么样的岁阳？
t_en: What kind of heliobus is Luxie?

t_ch: 这名字比其他岁阳幼稚一些。
t_en: Sounds like a childish name for a heliobus.

t_ch: 听着像是随口编的名字？
t_en: Sounds like a made-up name to me.

t_ch: 信不信由你，我可不像人类那般热爱谎言。
t_en: Disbelieve it if you like — that's up to you. Unlike humans, I get no joy from telling lies.

t_ch: 这家伙玩心极重，全不在意怎么操控凡人，一心只想着到处自由嬉戏。要说「燎原」分裂的碎片里，谁最可能干下诱拐你们家龙女的事情……也就只有它了。
t_en: Luxie loves mischief. They give no thought to interfering in mortal affairs — their only concerns are entertainment and freedom. Among all the fragments of Ignamar, the one most likely to abduct your precious Dragon Lady... would be Luxie.

t_ch: 封印千年至今，我们久久不曾品尝人类的喜怒哀乐，想必阿灼也一定迫不及待地到处溜达找个玩伴。
t_en: We were sealed away for a thousand years — it's been ages since we last tasted the joys and sorrows of the world of humans. I'm sure Luxie was eager to find a new friend and wander the world with them.

t_ch: 这坏东西的话多半是真的。岁阳的心性各不相同，全赖寄宿对象的性格熏染而成。
t_en: I think they're telling the truth... Heliobi do have different personalities, and they can be influenced by the personality of their host.

t_ch: 若是依附了好斗的战士，性格便渐渐好勇斗狠了起来；而要是遇上了玩心甚重的孩童，也就成了童心未泯的游灵。
t_en: If a heliobus possesses a great warrior, they might gradually take on a braver, fiercer persona... and if their host is a playful child, they may adopt a childlike spirit.

t_ch: 我瞧白露小姐经常逃家跑到金人巷来玩儿，感觉也是个渴望自由的小孩子啊。
t_en: Hmm... "wander the world with them." It sounds like Lady Bailu would've been an ideal target — she's always running off to play in Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 嗯，有了这条线索，事情办起来想必也能顺利多了。
t_en: Mm... This intel should be a big help.

t_ch: 喂！你们几个！赌约还没结束，再和我比一场！
t_en: Hey! We're not done here! You still owe me a fight!

t_ch: 之后的事之后再说吧。
t_en: We'll get to it sooner or later.

t_ch: 你横竖也赢不了。
t_en: You wouldn't win anyway.

t_ch: 等办完差再来收拾你喔！
t_en: We have to run some errands first — be patient!

t_ch: 混蛋，赢了区区一场就神气活现！再来，再来啊！
t_en: You bunch of meat bags! One minor victory and you think you're something special! Come on, show me what you've got!

t_ch: 事情有了眉目，镇伏岁阳之事还要拜托诸位快快行动起来。
t_en: The clues are all pointing in the same direction now. It would be wise to strike while the iron is hot.

t_ch: 多谢将军，咱们这就出发吧！
t_en: Thank you for your help, general. Let's get going!

t_ch: 好几天没回金人巷了，回到老地盘，感觉还真是亲切呀。
t_en: Ahh, I haven't been here for a while — it's nice to be back in the old stomping ground.

t_ch: 那带路和打听都靠小桂子这个地头蛇啦。
t_en: Oh yeah? Since you're so familiar with the place, you can lead the way and conduct the interviews!

t_ch: 跟我说相声呢？嘴可真够贫的！
t_en: Did I hear something from the peanut gallery?

t_ch: 瞧那边，有两个人穿着丹鼎司的制服，应该是来找龙女的吧……
t_en: Look! Those two are wearing Alchemy Commission uniforms. They're probably looking for the Dragon Lady...

t_ch: 嗯，龙女凭空消失，他们想必也是一筹莫展。不过，咱们打听消息时，最好别透露身份，也别提岁阳附身的事情…搞不好会造成恐慌……
t_en: Lady Bailu disappeared into thin air — they must be pretty confused. Remember, we have to keep our identities secret when we interview them... and don't mention anything about the heliobi — people might start panicking...

t_ch: 嗯嗯，判官大人你可放心，如何同群众有效交流可是云骑的必修课～
t_en: Don't worry Judge Huohuo. Effective communication with the public is one of the basic skills of a Cloud Knight~

t_ch: 二位好啊！看衣着想必是丹鼎司的医士，在下是云骑士卒素裳。请问两位是在寻找突然失踪的龙女大人吗？
t_en: Hello there! Judging by your clothing, you two must be healers from the Alchemy Commission. My name is Sushang — I'm a Cloud Knight Soldier. Are you looking for the Dragon Lady who suddenly disappeared?

t_ch: 呃…怎么连云骑都给惊动了？年轻，是你打了报告嘛？
t_en: Uh... Even the Cloud Knights are getting involved? Did you file a report or something?

t_ch: 老成，我可是连地衡司都没给通知啊。这事儿透着邪门，报告了人家都当你哗众取宠呢。不是，云骑是怎么知道这事儿的？
t_en: Report? I haven't said a word to the Realm-Keeping Commission, let alone the Cloud Knights. People would think we were crazy if we reported this... There's something fishy going on — how did the Cloud Knights find out?

t_ch: 呃…问得好。是这样的，咱们是隶属于十……
t_en: Uh... Hah, good question! So, actually, we're affiliated with the Ten—

t_ch: 十小队！这是被派来负责巡街应对各种突发事件的云骑治安第十小队！两位懂吧，就是专派些愣头青去干苦差事的那种小队。
t_en: The Tenth Squad! Cloud Knight Security Squad Ten, to be precise, hehe... We patrol the streets and respond to all kinds of unexpected incidents! It's grunt work, essentially...

t_ch: 懂的懂的，咱们这不也是被差来干苦差事吗？
t_en: I can relate. We're no stranger to grunt work ourselves.

t_ch: 总之，我们听说了龙女消失的事情，就赶来看看能不能帮上些忙。
t_en: Anyway, we heard about the Dragon Lady's disappearance, so we hurried over to see if we could help.

t_ch: 哎哟，这边这位{NICKNAME}我认得，是星穹列车的人。我印象里听龙女大人提过{F#她}{M#他}的。
t_en: Oh! We know this {NICKNAME} here — {M#he's}{F#she's} from the Astral Express. I've heard the Dragon Lady mention {M#him}{F#her} before.

t_ch: 咱俩可是忘年交。
t_en: She and I are best buddies.

t_ch: 我这么有名的吗？
t_en: Am I that famous?

t_ch: 没错，我是她的长期饭票！
t_en: That's right, I'm her long-term meal ticket!

t_ch: 你别给我乱说话！
t_en: Psst, be normal!

t_ch: 哎呀，你们肯帮忙，那是大大的好事。但我们俩其实还没搞清状况……
t_en: Well, it's great that you all are willing to help. But we haven't actually figured it out what exactly is going on...

t_ch: 是啊，我们本来已经抓到了龙女大人，嘿，没想到她砰地不见了。
t_en: Uh yeah, just when we had her cornered, the Dragon Lady disappeared with a "bang."

t_ch: 而且…我们在追龙女大人时，还看到了…两个她！真是莫名其妙啊。
t_en: And while we were chasing her, we saw two of her! It really is a mystery.

t_ch: 肯定是云吟术。
t_en: It must be cloudhymn magic.

t_ch: 我就说吧！…但云吟术真能做到这个效果吗？
t_en: That's what I was just saying! ...But can cloudhymn magic really do that?

t_ch: 能啊，我身边这位…你别看她是狐人啊，她是仙舟一级非物质文化遗产评定专家，她对云吟超懂的好不好。下面，请藿专家为我们讲两句。
t_en: It sure can! This foxian beside me... well, she's actually the Xianzhou's top expert on intangible cultural heritage — she's super knowledgeable about cloudhymn magic. Huohuo, could you share some expert insight with us?

t_ch: 嗯…嗯？啊对！我是专专专家…能办到的！别说云吟，咱们狐族人表演戏法时也会玩弄些狐火之术，隐去身形的！
t_en: Huh? Umm... Ah, yes! I—I'm a very professional expert... and I'd be happy to share my expert knowledge! In fact, Cloudhymn magic isn't the only magic out there that can achieve such feats — we foxians can use our foxfire to make ourselves invisible!

t_ch: 两位一定是眼花了。
t_en: Maybe you were experiencing double vision?

t_ch: 不至于啊，我俩加一块还不到五百岁呢。
t_en: I'm not even <unbreak>500</unbreak> years old yet! My eyes are as sharp as they come!

t_ch: 那就是工作太累了…丹鼎司医士救死扶伤，每天操劳过度，很容易产生幻觉的。作为仙舟行业道德的表率，您哥俩可得保重身体啊。
t_en: Hmm, maybe you're overworked. Alchemy Commission healers are saving people <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7 — I'd start seeing things too if I were that busy. As the face of medicinal mastery on the Xianzhou, you two need to be living healthier lifestyles.

t_ch: 小姑娘，你真是太懂我们了。等忙完这一阵，老成，咱哥俩得请个年假了。
t_en: Huh, you really get what we're up against... After this is behind us, I say we take a holiday and get rested up.

t_ch: 所以你们最后一次是在哪里见到的白露大人？
t_en: So where did you last see Lady Bailu?

t_ch: 就在旁边那条路啊。我总感觉那里有小孩儿在嬉笑的声音，是风声？
t_en: On that road there... and I swear I can hear a child giggling whenever I walk by. Must be the wind...

t_ch: 是错觉，这个红头发的姑娘说的一点儿也没错，咱们太累了。
t_en: You're just hearing things. That red-haired girl is right — we're overworked to the point of exhaustion.

t_ch: 两位好好歇着，由咱们帮你一块儿找找吧。白露小姐一定是瞧见你们到处找她，不敢出来了。有她的老朋友{NICKNAME}在，说不定她就愿意见人了呢？
t_en: Be sure to get some rest! We'll take over from here. Lady Bailu must have seen you and gone off to hide... but with her old friend {NICKNAME} here, maybe she'll be willing to come back out.

t_ch: 好的好的，那就麻烦你们了。
t_en: Fantastic. Well, we'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 白露小姐！白露小姐你在哪儿呀？
t_en: Lady Bailu! Lady Bailu, where are you?

t_ch: 龙女大人！出来一起玩儿啊！
t_en: Lady Bailu, come out and play with us!

t_ch: 这怎么有两…两个白露大人……
t_en: ...Wh—Why are there two of her?

t_ch: 他们在叫咱们一块玩儿呢！
t_en: They're asking us to play with them!

t_ch: 傻瓜，他们是骗你的！肯定是要把你抓回去呢！
t_en: Silly-head, they're trying to trick you! They're just gonna catch you and take you back!

t_ch: 咱们不如玩个游戏，「别让人抓到」！
t_en: I know! Let's play a game called "don't let anyone catch us!"

t_ch: 跑咯！
t_en: Run!

t_ch: 鬼啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！
t_en: Ghoooooooost!!!

t_ch: 姐姐你吓死我了…这两个一模一样的白露没惊到我，你嚎一嗓子差点没给我送走……
t_en: Seriously...? That ghost twin was one thing, but your screaming nearly sent me to the grave...

t_ch: 不好意思不好意思！说真的，虽然明知道这是岁阳作祟，但还是很吓人啊！
t_en: Ah, sorry! But seriously — I knew it was a heliobus, but seeing double Bailu like that sent shivers down my spine!

t_ch: 我、我完全理解素裳小姐…就算在十王司办差这么久，还是没法习惯这些妖怪搞出来的怪事。
t_en: I—It's okay, Miss. Sushang... I've worked in the Ten-Lords Commission for many years now, but I'm still not used to their tricks...

t_ch: 现在，怎么追？
t_en: How do we catch them now?

t_ch: 只是岁阳屏蔽了我们的感官而已…找找看吧，她们一定还在附近。
t_en: They're eluding our senses, but they won't have gone far... We just need to look around.

t_ch: 声音好像就是从这个房间里发出来的。我打开门看看啊，各位小心。
t_en: It seems to be coming from this room. I'll open the door and take a look. Everyone, stay on high alert.

t_ch: 嗯？好像打不开。
t_en: Huh? It won't open.

t_ch: （尝试开门）
t_en: (Try to open the door.)

t_ch: 你试着打开这扇门，但门却像焊死了一样纹丝不动。
t_en: You try to open the door but it won't budge.

t_ch: （尝试撞门）
t_en: (Try to ram the door open)

t_ch: 你试着撞开这扇门，但门却像焊死了一样纹丝不动。
t_en: You try to ram the door open but it won't budge.

t_ch: （尝试踢门）
t_en: (Try to kick the door down.)

t_ch: 你试着踢开这扇门，但门却像焊死了一样纹丝不动。你的脚很疼，非常疼。
t_en: You try to kick the door open, but it won't budge, and your feet hurt.

t_ch: 怪事…这难不成也是岁阳在作祟？
t_en: Strange... Is this also the work of the heliobus?

t_ch: 把来追它的人拒之于门外，好强的戒心。不过，既然知道它在哪儿出没，该是用上这个的时候了。
t_en: They must know that we're following... but now that we know its hiding spot, it's time to use this.

t_ch: 这个铃铛叫「同心火铃」，是绥园灾异后十王司捣鼓出来对付岁阳的道具。
t_en: This is the Harmonic Chime. It's an item that the Ten-Lords Commission forged to help deal with the heliobi after the Fyxestroll Garden incident.

t_ch: 岁阳会将无关人等排斥在幻境之外，独留受害者，但摇动这铃铛，能把咱们几人思考波长与岁阳同调。具体的原理我不多解释了，真要念说明书念完天也该黑了……
t_en: Heliobi create illusions to isolate their victims... but if we ring this chime, our thought-waves will synchronize with those of the heliobus. As for how it works... I'm afraid that would take the rest of the day to explain.

t_ch: 也就是说，现在的咱们被它当成了自己人，可以进到那家伙创造的幻境里去了？
t_en: In other words, we'll be able to enter the heliobus's illusion?

t_ch: 嗯，至于进去之后该怎么抓住那个阿灼，就要另想办法了。
t_en: Exactly... but once we're inside the illusion, we'll still have to figure out a way to catch Luxie.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们几个里就你最厉害，就由你来负责开门吧……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're the powerhouse of this team, we'll leave the door to you.

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: Doors are my specialty.

t_ch: 嗯…请一定要小心。
t_en: Mm... please be careful.

t_ch: 给我豁出去了是吧。
t_en: Why, how generous of you...

t_ch: 我不是这个意思！我只是信任你的能力。
t_en: Mm... be careful not to hurt yourself.

t_ch: 我三脚猫的功夫罢了。
t_en: I'm a jack of many trades, but master of none...

t_ch: 行了吧你，太谦虚可讨人嫌了啊。
t_en: Come on, you're too modest for your own good.

t_ch: 你准备好了就摇铃开门吧，我们一起冲进去。
t_en: When you're ready, ring the chime — we'll all charge in together.

t_ch: （摇动铃铛）
t_en: (Ring the chime)

t_ch: 我再准备一下。
t_en: I need a moment to get ready.

t_ch: 可别等太久啊，我担心她们又趁机逃到别处去。
t_en: Don't wait too long. They might make a run for it before we get there.

t_ch: 说句实话…阿灼似乎并不像印象中的其他岁阳那么危险。
t_en: Truth be told... Luxie didn't seem as evil as the other heliobi we've seen.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，这回除了运动量大了点，倒也没有到让人心跳停止的地步。不过要是多跑几圈，我得申请工伤了。
t_en: They sure made us run, though. If I ever have to run like that again on the job, I'm filing for worker's comp.

t_ch: 岁阳没有善恶之分，他们只是依照自己的欲望和情绪来行动。希望收服其他的岁阳时，也能这么轻松。
t_en: Heliobi don't really fall under "good" or "evil" — they just act according to their desires and emotions. I hope the others will be just as easy to subdue.

t_ch: 我也玩得蛮开心的。
t_en: I had fun.

t_ch: 开心啥啊，累死我了。
t_en: I nearly had a heart attack.

t_ch: 事情圆满解决就好。
t_en: Just glad it's all over.

t_ch: 我也该去向两位辛苦找我的医助投案自首去了。
t_en: I should go surrender myself to those two medics — they were looking so hard to find me...

t_ch: 等等！
t_en: Wait!

t_ch: 嗯？还有什么事吗？
t_en: Huh? What is it?

t_ch: 我们要把这个「灵异事件」的调查报告发到网上，来吸引更多粉丝为我们提供关于岁阳的消息……
t_en: We're going to make a post about this "paranormal incident" online to attract more followers — it'll help us get more intel.

t_ch: 你们向白露解释了「罗浮杂俎」的事。
t_en: You tell Bailu about the website Ghostly Grove

t_ch: 我明白了，不宜对外宣布「龙女被岁阳附体」，所以得隐去我的名字，另编一个夸张的标题？
t_en: Oh, I get it... We can't just tell the public that the Dragon Lady was possessed by a heliobus, right? So... we won't mention my name right? And we'll make up a headline?

t_ch: 没错，我们想把这件事渲染成灵异现象，越夸张越好，然后再对外宣称已被咱们这个小队顺利解决了！配上照片视频若干，一定能抓人眼球，换来更多的消息。
t_en: That's right, we wanna report this incident like it's a paranormal phenomenon — the more exaggerated the better — and then announce that it was solved by our ghost-hunting squad! Then we add on a few photos and videos — it'll be sure to get lots of views!

t_ch: 唔…这倒是没问题，只是我不知道该怎么编呢……
t_en: Um... That's great and all, but I'm not good at making stuff up on the spot...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！我之前在西衍先生那儿听过你联名创作的评书，感觉你挺擅长胡编乱造的！
t_en: {NICKNAME} I heard that you helped Mr. Xiyan write stories before — you must be an expert wordsmith!

t_ch: 你对我误会太深了！
t_en: I'm afraid you're overestimating me...

t_ch: 这个账号不是你在经营吗？
t_en: Aren't you the one in charge of social media engagement?

t_ch: 群策群力嘛，快帮我想想法子！
t_en: It's all a team effort. Come on, help me think of something!

t_ch: 嗯…这个标题不错，各位觉得呢？那就这么决定了。
t_en: Hmmm... I say that's a good title. What does everyone think? Alrighty then, it's decided.

t_ch: 请白露小姐也早些回去，别让那两位医助等太久，他们也挺不容易的。
t_en: You should go back soon, Lady Bailu. Those two medics have had a rough day.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，谢谢几位。我先走啦～
t_en: Okay, I'm going back now. Thanks for the rescue~

t_ch: 总算是解决了一件岁阳危机！原来抓岁阳是这么累的一件事啊……
t_en: Case closed! I never knew catching heliobi would require so much work...

t_ch: 事情还没完全办妥。按照寒鸦大人的吩咐，咱们一旦收服了岁阳，就要立刻赶回绥园，把它锁进「束形却邪阵」的镇妖塔里。
t_en: We're not done yet... Madam Hanya said that once we've subdued a heliobus, we should return to Fyxestroll Garden and lock them inside one of the Evil Binding Matrix's suppression towers.

t_ch: 不知道网友们会作何反应，你们明白，再过段时间「罗浮杂俎」就会热闹起来。
t_en: I'm not sure how the online community will react, but you can bet the Ghostly Grove will be the talk of the town sooner or later.

t_ch: 这下终于彻底解决了！呼…可以好好休息一下了。
t_en: It's over! Whew... Now I can finally get some rest.

t_ch: 不过，游荡在外面的岁阳还有很多很多…我们也休息不了多久……
t_en: But there are still lots of heliobi roaming around out there... we can't rest for too long.

t_ch: 明日事就让明日愁吧。
t_en: Let tomorrow worry about tomorrow.

t_ch: 深有同感。
t_en: I couldn't agree more.

t_ch: 哦～咱这个账号粉丝数量猛涨啊！
t_en: Ooooh⁓! Our follower count has skyrocketed!

t_ch: 粉丝数多起来，果然就能收到一些新线索…你们看，我的战略没有错！
t_en: And as predicted, we got some new leads... Looks like my strategy is working like a treat!

t_ch: 等大伙休息得差不多了，就去看看这些线索吧。搞不好背后就有岁阳在作乱。
t_en: When everyone's rested and ready, let's go through the leads. Time to find another heliobus.

t_ch: 现在还是专心休息吧…抓岁阳的事，还是等我的心肺功能缓过劲来再说吧……
t_en: I'd rather focus on resting first... I need to wait for my heart rate to recover before I go chasing another heliobus.

t_ch: 捉鬼小队解散之后，聊天框开始闪动起来…
t_en: After the ghost-hunting squad disbanded, the chat starts to flash with activity...

t_ch: 剩下的事，一会在群里聊吧。
t_en: Let's continue in a group chat.

t_ch: 记得回去分享一下账号情况。
t_en: Remember to share the account updates.

t_ch: 无聊到令人心碎，连偶尔有飞虫掠过都成了乐趣。
t_en: This boredom is killing me... the only entertainment I have around here is watching the insects fly by.

t_ch: 啊，有只虫子飞来了，嗡嗡嗡，{F#她}{M#他}是来嘲笑我这束火焰，还是打算浪费唾沫星子试图用人类的道德感化我？就让咱们拭目以待吧……
t_en: Speaking of which, it seems one of them has come to visit me. I wonder, is {F#she}{M#he} here to taunt me, or implement some sort of restorative justice? We'll just have to wait and see...

t_ch: 还没放弃挣扎吗？
t_en: Still haven't given up?

t_ch: 十王司的判官既不打算抛弃自己的同僚，又不打算放我离开。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission judges won't abandon one of their own... but they won't let me off the hook either.

t_ch: 于是事情就成了无聊的耐力比拼。嘻嘻，不知道是仙舟人的寿命更长，还是岁阳的耐心更顽固……
t_en: So now I'm stuck here, condemned to eternal boredom. Hehe, I wonder which will last longer — the lifespan of my jailors, or the patience of a heliobus...

t_ch: 时间站在我这一边，只要仙舟人还不想扯下人性这块遮羞布。
t_en: In any case, time is on my side, so long as the Xianzhou are willing to maintain some facade of humanity.

t_ch: 要怎样你才肯投降？
t_en: What are your terms of surrender?

t_ch: 投降？我为什么要投降？在你眼中，我只是扣住了这个冥差作为残子，徒劳地负隅顽抗。
t_en: Surrender? Why would I do that? To you, I may as well be fighting an unwinnable battle — wasting my time away in someone else's body...

t_ch: 但在我眼里，这局棋还没到将死的一步呢，十王司无奈我何。棋局尚有翻盘的余地。
t_en: ...but for me, the game has only just begun. The Ten-Lords Commission doesn't have any moves left... and I still have time to turn the game in my favor.

t_ch: 何况，在不久前你和那些判官差点就成了我的玩偶——要不是那条看门狗不知所谓地搅局……
t_en: You know, it wasn't that long ago that you and those judges would have become my pawns — if it wasn't for that meddling guard dog...

t_ch: 我无话可说。
t_en: I'm speechless.

t_ch: 既然无话可说，就别烦我了，小东西。
t_en: If you have nothing to say, then run along and let me be.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，见到您一切安好，甚是可喜。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, it is good to see you are doing well.

t_ch: 绥园灾异一事，我受姐姐命令前去请回援助。在此期间，多亏了您在园中撑持，才令妖祸不致泛滥，小女子须向您致谢。
t_en: During the Fyxestroll Garden incident, my sister gave the order to seek reinforcements. It was thanks to your support in that critical moment that we were able to get the situation under control. I wanted to thank you.

t_ch: 而今这支小队伍，也需要您的帮助。
t_en: And now this small team needs your help, too.

t_ch: 雪衣怎么不在？
t_en: Where's Xueyi?

t_ch: 姐姐在之前的战斗中，损伤甚重。
t_en: She sustained heavy injuries in the previous incident.

t_ch: 所幸姐姐是以机巧之身还阳行事。若回到棺中，由冥差们维护更新，不至于有损性命。但我依然很担心她。
t_en: Fortunately, my sister is using an ingenium body. As long as she returns to the coffin and is tended to by the spiritfarers, her life is not in jeopardy. I still worry about her, though.

t_ch: 血肉之躯会畏惧死伤，想要保全自己，但姐姐不会。她正渐渐脱离自己为人时的常识，将自己视为「工具」，毫不体恤地消耗自己的身体。
t_en: Creatures of flesh and blood fear death and will always strive to preserve their life... but not my sister. Her human instinct for survival is slipping away — she sees herself more as a "tool," driving her body to the point of total exhaustion.

t_ch: 每当看到她战斗，我都深感不忍。
t_en: Every time she fights like that, I can't bear to watch.

t_ch: 我们真的能比十王司更专业？
t_en: Can we really handle this better than the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 在其他事情上也许未必，但在岁阳一事上，答案已经很明了了。
t_en: In most matters, I would say no. But when dealing with heliobi... I think the answer is quite clear.

t_ch: 罗浮十王司长久以来专攻镇伏孽物，却对洪炉的危险疏于防范。此次变生腋肘，冥差与金人对岁阳无力拮抗，证明过去的伏魔手段已经不奏效了。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission have long specialized in suppressing abominations, but they were negligent in guarding against the dangers of the Creation Furnace. Once it became a problem, the spiritfarers and aurumatons were powerless against the heliobi, proving that the traditional means of subduing them were no longer effective.

t_ch: 反倒是许多年前我们无意间救下的女孩，成了击败浮烟的关键。世事真是奇妙。
t_en: As fate would have it, the girl we unwittingly rescued many years ago became the key to defeating Cirrus.

t_ch: 此事之后，我们打算与朱明仙舟通信。那座仙舟受帝弓神话的影响，人们与岁阳共生，又能不受其左右。吾等当能从那儿求得抵御岁阳之术。
t_en: After this is all settled, we intend to make contact with the Xianzhou Zhuming. That ship is under the influence of the mythical Reignbow Arbiter — the people there are able to coexist with the heliobi and at the same time be free from their manipulation. We hope that from them we can learn the art of breaking free from the influence of the heliobi.

t_ch: 十王司真的在…阴间？
t_en: Is it true that the Ten-Lords Commission is located in... the netherworld?

t_ch: 您真把我们当成与阳世不通音信的幽冥来客么？这份天真可爱得紧。
t_en: Did you really think we were messengers from another plane visiting the world of the living? Hehe, how cute of you.

t_ch: 就当是吧。与小女子靠得太近，会有损阳气喔。
t_en: And maybe we are... don't get too close, or you might turn into a ghost too.

t_ch: 十王司所在之地，是绝对的秘密。您为仙舟贡献良多，离这个秘密已然很近了。若想进一步知道，「捉鬼小队」的事情，要劳您多多费心了。
t_en: The precise location of the Ten-Lords Commission is highly confidential... but you have contributed much to the Xianzhou and are very close to the secret. If you wish to know more about it, I will have to ask you to spend time with our ghost-hunting squad.

t_ch: 明白，我就是仙舟奇兵队长。
t_en: This has to be the strangest group of people on the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 您说笑了。所谓能者多劳，多劳多得，此番盟助，十王司也会重重酬谢。
t_en: I would save the jokes for later! As the saying goes, with great work comes great rewards. The Ten-Lords Commission will compensate you accordingly.

t_ch: 小女子时常也在想，罗浮欠下无名客如许恩情，该拿什么来回报？
t_en: I've wondered at times what the Luofu should give the Nameless in return for their great deeds...

t_ch: 思来想去，实在没有答案。毕竟这可是十王与将军才能操心的事情。
t_en: Alas, questions like that are above my pay grade. That is for the Ten Lords and the General to figure out.

t_ch: 藿藿和尾巴…
t_en: About Huohuo and Tail...

t_ch: 十王司判官的过去原是一律被抹去的，一入幽府，便不牵外缘了。
t_en: A judge of the Ten-Lords Commission has no past. Once one has entered the service of the commission, all links with the outside world are severed...

t_ch: 不过，你眼下已是这支小队的一员了，若要彼此合作，那么了解自然是少不得的一环。我就和你谈谈自己所知的一些秘辛旧事好了。
t_en: However, since you are now a member of the squad, and in the interest of everyone working together, it's best that we know each other a little better... — I'll tell you some of the secrets I know.

t_ch: 自从「燎原」与将军大战落败后，十王司受命封印分灵碎片。大部分岁阳都被收入造化洪炉，为其贻害而赎罪。
t_en: After Ignamar was defeated by the general in a great battle, the Ten-Lords Commission was ordered to seal away the heliobi fragments. To make them atone for their actions, most of the heliobi were sealed inside the Creation Furnace.

t_ch: 数十年前，十王司收到请求，有个狐人小姑娘的尾巴被一团无源怪火给吞噬了，于是我等奉命出巡，镇伏妖物。
t_en: Decades ago, the Ten-Lords Commission was asked to investigate an unusual case — a foxian girl's tail had been consumed by a strange fire of unknown origin... so we set out, ready to subdue whatever foul demon awaited us.

t_ch: 待我们赶到，才察觉这团火正是逃逸在外的岁阳。不知为何，「燎原」的分灵碎片中仍有漏网之鱼。
t_en: It turned out that the fire was none other than a heliobus. The remaining fragments of Ignamar — the heliobi — had found some way to escape from their furnace prison.

t_ch: 它的力量本已衰弱之极，却因小姑娘的一念之善而被留存了下来。它渐渐恢复了心智，重又燃起了试图操控人类的念头。
t_en: That heliobus's power was weakened to the brink of death, but he was kept alive thanks to the kindness of the foxian girl. Gradually, he regained his strength and rekindled his desire to manipulate humans.

t_ch: 我本该将岁阳连根祓除，送入洪炉。可惜那时藿藿的身子骨太过衰弱，为了能保住她的生命，我只能书写符敕，将那东西暂时镇伏在她体内。
t_en: Normally, I would have just purged the heliobus and sent it back into the furnace. But Huohuo's body was too frail and weak to perform a purging. The best I could do without harming her was to inscribe a talisman seal — enough to subdue the thing living inside her, at least for a while.

t_ch: 之后十王司将藿藿招募为冥差学徒，学习驱魔捉鬼的本事，这不仅是为了看护藿藿，也是为了时刻观察她是否有被夺舍的情况。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission took Huohuo in as a spiritfarer apprentice, teaching her the arts of purgation and ghost hunting. This way the Commission could keep an eye on Huohuo — and ensure that she wasn't taken over by the heliobus.

t_ch: 但是尾巴不太像敌人。
t_en: But Tail doesn't seem like an enemy.

t_ch: 也许这些年与藿藿漫长的相处，让它学会了人类怜惜的情感。虽然尾巴一直没有放弃过挣脱束缚或篡噬寄主，但至少它为了藿藿对抗了同族。
t_en: Perhaps all those long years with Huohuo have bestowed upon that heliobus some form of human compassion. Though Tail has never given up on attempting to break free of his bonds or usurping his host, he has fought against his own kind to protect Huohuo.

t_ch: 尾巴却背叛了同族。
t_en: Tail betrayed his own kind.

t_ch: 是啊，谁能想到尾巴牺牲自己战胜了浮烟呢？虽说岁阳之间没有同心同德的必然，但他愿意站在藿藿这边，已经说明了很多事情。
t_en: You could say that. Who would have thought Tail would have sacrificed himself to defeat Cirrus? Granted, heliobi have no obligation to be loyal to one another, but Tail's willingness to side with Huohuo is significant.

t_ch: 「捉鬼小队」的事情，要劳您多多费心了。若有什么疑问，藿藿可代小女子解答。
t_en: I leave the matters of the ghost-hunting squad in your hands. If you have further questions, just ask Huohuo.

t_ch: 小女子身为文职，要操劳案牍之事去了。唉唉，做不完，真的做不完啊……
t_en: As always, I have mountains of paperwork to finish... Piles of paper... I'll never get it all done...

t_ch: 小桂子，这回是公务，不比咱们平时在街头那些小打小闹。你可别再那么兴奋了——
t_en: Little Gui, this is official business — it's nothing like the street fights we're always dealing with. You can stop being so excited...

t_ch: 裳裳，你就放一百个心吧。现在的我，冷静地就像一台无情的冰箱！
t_en: Don't worry, Sushang. Right now, I'm cool as a cucumber inside a block of ice...!

t_ch: 听说你上一回打碎了十王司的法器，差一些……
t_en: I heard that you broke one of the Ten-Lords Commission's sacred instruments, and almost—

t_ch: 上一回被那坏东西操纵，是因为…呃是因为措手不及啊，没来得及提气防御！不许再说上一回啦，还不兴人家将功补过嘛。
t_en: Uhh, I was possessed, okay? That thing snuck up on me — I couldn't even react! I'll make up for it this time.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得这样搜索岁阳可行吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you think we can really catch heliobi this way?

t_ch: 网络是很强大的。
t_en: The Internet is a very powerful tool.

t_ch: 但愿如此吧……
t_en: I sure hope so...

t_ch: 感觉不是很靠谱。
t_en: We'll be trusting random strangers...

t_ch: 是啊，感觉不是很靠谱……
t_en: That's what I'm worried about too — doesn't feel very reliable...
